title: Sedum atypicum
taxonomy: Sedum atypicum is classified within the plant family Crassulaceae. It belongs to the genus Sedum and is a type of succulent plant. Sedum atypicum is further categorized as a species within this genus. This species is native to China and is also known as the 'Ryukyu stonecrop'. Sedum atypicum is closely related to other Sedum species, sharing characteristics such as thick, fleshy leaves and stems adapted for water storage.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in China, where it is believed to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
